source: Learning bash with Rocky - Conditional Structures (if and case): if / then / else / elif / fi and parameter substitution

questions.q: In `if command; then ... fi`, what decides which branch runs?
options: The command's return code ($?): 0 runs then, non-zero runs else
questions.why: if evaluates the return code of the command placed after it.

questions.q: Which keyword closes an if structure?

questions.why: You often act only when the test is true; else is optional.

questions.q: How do you merge an else immediately followed by a new if?
questions.why: elif combines else and if into one branch.

questions.q: What does `[[ -e /etc/passwd ]] && echo yes || echo no` do?
options: Runs echo yes if the test is true, echo no if it is false
questions.why: && runs the command on true; || runs the (optional) command on false.

options: Displays value if the variable is empty (without assigning it)
questions.why: :- substitutes a replacement value when the variable is empty, without changing it.

options: Assigns value to the variable if it is empty
questions.why: := assigns a new value when the variable is empty.

options: Displays value if the variable is NOT empty
questions.why: :+ substitutes a value when the variable is set/non-empty.
